body.purpose: Default UI text, capped at 65-75ch for prose.

shadows.name: lg
shadows.purpose: Modal panels, the deepest functional layer.

shadows.name: glow-accent
shadows.purpose: Signal glow for primary action hover/focus only.

motion.name: base
motion.purpose: Default state transitions (button hover, chip state).

motion.name: slow
motion.purpose: Modal/panel entrance and larger surface changes.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Signal-amber primary action button with glow on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 7px; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1; padding: 8px 16px; background: #f5a623; border: 1px solid #f5a623; color: #1a1205; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, box-shadow 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #ffb53d; border-color: #ffb53d; box-shadow: 0 0 0 1px #f5a62333, 0 6px 20px #f5a62322; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffb53d; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Default low-emphasis button, transparent at rest.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 7px; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1; padding: 8px 16px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #27272b; color: #9a9aa3; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease, color 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: #1d1d21; border-color: #34343a; color: #f2f2f3; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #ffb53d; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Danger Button
components.description: Destructive action button, red on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-danger { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 7px; border-radius: 8px; font-weight: 600; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1; padding: 8px 16px; background: transparent; border: 1px solid #e5484d66; color: #e5484d; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.18s ease, border-color 0.18s ease; } .ds-btn-danger:hover { background: #2a1012; border-color: #e5484d; } .ds-btn-danger:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #e5484d; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Chip
components.css: .ds-chip { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 5px; padding: 3px 9px; border-radius: 999px; background: #141417; border: 1px solid #27272b; color: #9a9aa3; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; line-height: 1.4; } .ds-chip-dot { width: 6px; height: 6px; border-radius: 50%; background: #e5484d; }

components.name: Pill
components.description: Tinted-background status pill with uppercase label, stronger emphasis than Chip.
components.css: .ds-pill { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 999px; background: #2ab3d41f; color: #2ab3d4; font-size: 10px; font-weight: 700; letter-spacing: 0.4px; text-transform: uppercase; }

components.name: Card
components.description: Standard content container, flat at rest.
components.html:
Session Summary
Meeting notes and AI-generated highlights appear here.
components.css: .ds-card { background: #141417; border: 1px solid #27272b; border-radius: 8px; padding: 16px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-card-title { color: #f2f2f3; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600; } .ds-card-body { color: #9a9aa3; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; }

components.name: Modal Panel
components.description: Overlay dialog panel, deepest elevation layer.
components.html:
End Session?

This will stop recording and generate the post-meeting summary.
components.css: .ds-modal-backdrop { position: fixed; inset: 0; background: rgba(0,0,0,0.66); backdrop-filter: blur(2px); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 24px; } .ds-modal-panel { width: 440px; max-width: 100%; background: #1a1a1e; border: 1px solid #34343a; border-radius: 12px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 16px 40px rgba(0,0,0,0.50); } .ds-modal-title { color: #f2f2f3; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 600; margin: 0 0 18px; } .ds-modal-body { color: #9a9aa3; font-size: 13px; line-height: 1.5; } .ds-modal-footer { display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; gap: 8px; margin-top: 22px; }

components.name: Empty State
components.css: .ds-empty { display: flex; flex-direction: column; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 48px 24px; border: 1px dashed #27272b; border-radius: 8px; color: #5c5c64; font-size: 13px; text-align: center; gap: 8px; } .ds-empty-icon { font-size: 20px; opacity: 0.4; }

narrative.overview: Stratis reads like an instrument panel for a live conversation, not a marketing surface. Surfaces sit at near-black (#09090b) and step up in small, deliberate increments — bg to surface to surfaceHover to surfaceElevated — so the facilitator can tell at a glance what layer they're looking at without any of it competing for attention. One signal color, amber, is reserved for what actually needs the facilitator's eyes: a primary action, an active recording state, an AI suggestion worth noticing. Everything else recedes into muted grays so the signal stays a signal. This system explicitly rejects generic SaaS-dashboard scaffolding: no hero-metric tiles, no gradient text, no tiny uppercase eyebrows stacked above every section, no identical icon-card grids. It also rejects marketing-surface bleed — Stratis's core pages are a working tool used during a live meeting, not a pitch to be admired, so density and legibility win over decorative flourish.
keyCharacteristics: Near-black base with amber as the only saturated color in the system
Layered surface elevation communicates structure, not decoration
Small, deliberate hover/active feedback — nothing playful or bouncy
Semantic colors (teal, green, red, orange, cyan) exist only for status meaning, never as decoration

rules.name: The One Signal Rule
rules.body: Amber is the only saturated, attention-seeking color in the system. If a screen has more than one element competing for attention via color, one of them is wrong — reach for weight, size, or position before reaching for a second bright color.

rules.name: The No-Drama Pairing Rule
rules.body: One family (Inter), weight and size carry all hierarchy. Do not introduce a second display face — it fights the Control Room's instrument-panel restraint.

rules.name: The Structural Shadow Rule
rules.body: A shadow's depth must match a real layering relationship (this sits above that). Never add a shadow purely to make an element feel important — use the amber glow for that instead.

dos: Do keep amber (#f5a623) as the only saturated, attention-seeking color on any given screen — the One Signal Rule.
Do use surface layering (Void, Panel, Panel Elevated) to communicate structure, matching a real layering relationship.
Do keep hover/active feedback small and deliberate — border or background shift, glow only on primary actions.
Do reserve the amber glow() for primary actions and focus states, not general decoration.
Do cap body text line length around 65-75ch and keep body text at #f2f2f3 or #9a9aa3 minimum for contrast.
Do support prefers-reduced-motion alternatives for any animation.
donts: Don't introduce a second saturated/bright accent color competing with amber on the same screen.
Don't use hero-metric tiles, gradient text, tiny uppercase eyebrows above every section, or identical icon-card grids.
Don't let marketing/campaign visual language bleed into the core facilitator/meeting product surfaces.
Don't add shadows purely for decorative importance — depth must reflect a real layering relationship.
Don't use bounce/elastic easing anywhere; motion here is functional feedback, not personality.
Don't darken Ink Muted (#9a9aa3) back toward the old #666 — it was lifted specifically for legibility.
